title: Planetary variator for variable transmission
abstract: The invention is related to a planetary variator applicable in a variable transmission for realizing a variable speed and torque ratio, the variator comprising a ring wheel (16), at least two planet wheels (19) and a sun wheel (17) the interaction between the wheels takes place through an essentially drill-free rolling motion of a rolling surface provided on each of the planet wheels' wheel portions (12), on rolling surfaces (20, 21) provided on the ring wheel (16) and the sun wheel (17), and wherein each planet wheel (19) is freely rotatable about a hinge axis (24) wherein said hinge axis (24) is oriented perpendicularly with respect to the plane formed by the planet wheel's rotation axis (25) and the common central axis (18), while being external to the common central axis (18).
claims:
Claims 1. A planetary variator applicable in a variable transmission for realizing a variable speed and torque ratio, the variator comprising a ring wheel (16), at least two planet wheels (19) and a sun wheel (17), wherein: Â· the ring wheel and the sun wheel are axisymmetric bodies positioned with respect to a common central symmetry axis (18), â¢ the planet wheels (19) comprise a shaft portion (14) and a wheel portion (12) that is rotatable about the shaft portion, the shaft portion having a longitudinal central axis (25), said axis being also the rotation axis of the wheel portion (12), Â· each planet wheel (19) is freely rotatable about a hinge axis (24) that is oriented essentially perpendicularly with respect to the plane defined by the common central axis (18) and the rotation axis (25) of the wheel portion (12) of the planet wheel (19), characterized in that : Â· the interaction between the wheels takes place through a rolling motion of a rolling surface provided on each of the planet wheels' wheel portions (12), on rolling surfaces (20,21 ) provided on the ring wheel (16) and the sun wheel (17), wherein the two planes (26,27) which are tangential to the rolling surfaces (20,21 ) of the ring wheel and the sun wheel, in the tangential contact points with a planet wheel (19), intersect with the rotation axis (25) of the wheel portion (12) of said planet wheel (19) in a point (28) of the common central axis (18), regardless of the angular position of said planet wheel (19) about the hinge axis (24), â¢ the cross-section of the rolling surface of the planet wheels by the plane defined by the central axis (18) and the longitudinal axis (25) of the planet wheel's shaft portion (14) is formed by at least portions of a first and second curve (35,36), wherein the first curve is tangential to the cross-section of the sun wheel (17) and the second curve is tangential to the cross-section of the ring wheel (16), and wherein the centres of curvature (C1 ,C2) of these curves in the tangential contact points (A1 ,A2) of said first and second curve with the sun and ring wheel cross- sections are not located on the hinge axis (24), â¢ the hinge axis (24) of each of the planet wheels (19) is external to the common central axis (18). 2. The planetary variator according to claim 1 , wherein the hinge axis (24) intersects with the rotation axis (25) of the planet wheel (19). 3. The planetary variator according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the hinge axis (24) is located between the wheel portion (12) of the planet wheel (19) and the intersection point (28) of the planet wheel rotation axis (25) and the central axis (18). 4. The planetary variator according to claim 3, wherein the variator comprises a central shaft (15) provided with a radial extension (30) and wherein the shaft portion (14) of the planet wheels (19) is rotatably connected to the radial extension through a hinge joint (23). 5. The planetary variator according to claim 4, wherein the shaft portion (14) of the planet wheels (19) is provided with a fork (22) being rotatable about the hinge joint (23), with respect to the radial extension (30). 6. The planetary variator according to claim 5, wherein the fork (22) has a main body (33), a central leg portion (31 ) and two side leg portions (32), the three leg portions extending away from the main body (33) and being connected to the hinge joint (23). 7. The planetary variator according to claim 1 or 2 wherein the hinge axis (24) is located beyond the wheel portion (12) of the planet wheel, as seen from the intersection point (28) between the planet wheel rotation axis 25 and the central axis (18). 8. The planetary variator according to claim 7, wherein the variator comprises a support wheel (40) mounted coaxially with the ring wheel (16) and the sun wheel (17) and wherein the shaft portion (14) of the planet wheel is rotatably connected to the support wheel through a hinge joint (41 ). 9. The planetary variator according to claim 8, wherein the hinge joint is incorporated in the support wheel (40). 10. The planetary variator according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the rolling surface of the wheel portion (12) of at least one of the planet wheels (19) has the shape of a circle arc, as seen in a cross section with a plane through the rotation axis (25) of said wheel portion. 11. The planetary variator according to any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein the rolling surface of the wheel portion (12) of at least one of the planet wheels (19) has a non-circular shape, as seen in a cross section with a plane through the rotation axis (25) of said wheel portion. 12. The planetary variator according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the wheel portion (12) of at least one of the planet wheels (19) rotates about the shaft portion (14) via one or more bearings (13,13'), said bearing or bearings 

 having an inner ring and an outer ring, and wherein the wheel portion (12) is mounted directly onto the outer ring of at least one of the bearings. 13. The planetary variator according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the wheel portion (12) of the planet wheel (19) rotates about the shaft portion (14) via one or more bearings (13,13'), said bearing or bearings having an inner ring and an outer ring, and wherein the wheel portion (12) forms a single piece with the outer ring of at least one of the bearings. 14. A planetary variator according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the centres of curvature (C1 ,C2) of said first and second curve (35,36) in said tangential contact points (A1 ,A2) are not coinciding in a point on the planet wheel's rotation axis (25). 15. A planetary variator according to any one of claims 1 to 13, wherein the centres of curvature (C1 ,C2) of said first and second curve (35,36) in said tangential contact points are coinciding in a point on the planet wheel's rotation axis (25). 16. A variable or reversible variable transmission comprising one or more planetary variators according to any one of the preceding claims.
